text: Galileo Galilei was born in Pisa, Italy in 1564. He studied at the University of Pisa and later taught at the University of Padua. Galileo improved the telescope and made key astronomical observations including the moons of Jupiter. He supported the Copernican heliocentric model and was tried by the Roman Inquisition. His work Dialogue Concerning the Two Chief World Systems was published in 1632.
